full_text:
The first major heat wave warning...Hot disease red light Body sensitivity temperature 38 degrees Elderly death risk 19%↑
Along with the nationwide heat wave warning, the first "heat wave severe warning" was issued in some parts of southern Gyeongsangbuk-do. A severe heat wave warning is issued when the maximum daily temperature is 38 degrees Celsius or the maximum daily temperature is 3 degrees Celsius or higher in areas where the maximum daily body temperature of 35 degrees Celsius or higher has been observed for more than two days.
The Korea Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said on the 12th, "If the body temperature rises above 38 degrees Celsius, the risk of death of the elderly increases by 19%, so please follow health rules to prevent heat diseases."
Heatwaves not only directly cause heat diseases such as heat stroke and heat exhaustion, but also worsen existing diseases such as cardio-cerebrovascular diseases, respiratory diseases, and kidney diseases, increasing the risk of hospitalization and death. In particular, special attention is needed in the severe heat wave warning stage, as healthy people can develop severe heat diseases during outdoor activities or work.
When the wind chill reaches 38 degrees Celsius, which is the standard for severe heat waves, it is analyzed that under the age of 65, the overall risk of death increases by 4% and the risk of cardiovascular disease increases by 7%.
It was found that the overall risk of death (including accidents and non-accidents) of the elderly aged 65 or older increased by 19% and the risk of cardiovascular death by 14%. In particular, the health risk from heat waves was relatively greater in the elderly.
The Korea Centers for Disease Control and Prevention emphasized, "In areas where a major heat wave warning has been issued, rice paddy and field work, construction site work, sports activities, and outdoor events should be immediately stopped or postponed, moved to a cool and shaded place, and checked the safety of family members and neighboring neighbors."
He added that they should actively practice health rules against heat waves, such as drinking water often, taking a sufficient rest in a cool environment, and refraining from outdoor activities during hot times.
